messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The mystery of how grace is not law is a foundational truth, yet it is often obscured by a cOLD religious mindset that tries to mix the two. The reasoning is simple: God is not the author of confusion, but of peace [^1]. Law and grace are two contrary principles that cannot co-exist in peace; one worketh wrath, the other worketh life. To think God is both Law and Grace is to imagine a bi-polar oxymoron, as if confusion, not peace, were the result [^1]. The apostle Paul helps us reason that because "law" worketh "wrath" (Rom 4:15), it is something to "put off" and "put away" (Eph 4:31; Col 3:8) [^1]. For where no law is, there is no transgression, and sin is not imputed when there is no law (Rom 4:8; 5:13) [^1]. We are not under the law, but under grace (Rom 6:14) [^1].

Consider the contrast: the global cult, the cabal, operates within a set of "rules" or spiritual principles they must follow to be allowed to exist [^2]. They understand that the universe is benevolent and will not allow the negative to succeed unless it is invited [^2]. This is a law-based operation—a system of works, permissions, and occult principles. But the God of all grace is not law, nor both law and grace, but all grace [^1]. Grace does not force itself on anyone; it is a gift that requires us to reckon ourselves dead to sin to be alive unto God [^1]. The Spirit of truth is sent to guide us through this minefield of mystery, replacing ignorance with revelation [^1].

So how do we spot the difference? A psyop, or a law operation, is identified by one thing: if the opinion coming out needs people to be silenced, it is a psyop [^3]. Law demands silence, conformity, and public shaming of those who question it [^3]. Grace, on the other hand, invites inquiry, study, and a personal reckoning. False flags promote the lie that the "Rule of Law" equals peace and safety, but the biblical truth is that law worketh wrath, not peace nor safety [^4]. The ThirdDay is our decision time about TheLastDay: will it be the Day of the LORD (LAW) = darkness, not light? Or will it be the Day of ThatGod = light having no darkness? [^4] Grace is the focus on the positive outcome, not the effort or the cost [^5]. It is the habit of thinking about the payoff—the delicious food, the awesome family—rather than the pain of childbirth [^5]. This is the diligence of grace, not the laziness of law.

Learn More:
- [God of all grace is not law, nor both law & grace, but all grace](/pages/god_of_all_grace_is_not_law_nor_both_law_&_grace_but_all_grace)
- [they know about the mystery of how grace is not law](/pages/ig_world_school_family_they_know_about_the_mystery_of_how_grace_is_not_law_20260411)
- [A Syop is a law operation and how to spot it as people has to be silenced](/pages/fb_chase_hughes_a_syop_is_a_law_operation_and_how_to_spot_it_as_people_has_to_be_silenced_20251114)
- [Law Worketh Wrath Is Not Peace Nor safety](/pages/law_worketh_wrath_is_not_peace_nor_safety)
- [diligence is not laziness (focus on grace)](/pages/tt_thinktobecome_diligence_is_not_laziness_focus_on_grace_20260324)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

messages.content: why Paul mentioned "the rulers of the darkness"?

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

Paul mentions "the rulers of the darkness of this world" (Eph 6:12) to contrast the two opposing spiritual systems: law and grace. Darkness, in the allegoric mystery, represents ignorance—specifically ignorance of God's grace [^1]. The "rulers" of such darkness are not literal demons with pitchforks, but rather the principles and powers of the law, which worketh wrath and keep souls in bondage to sin and death [^2]. Paul himself was once a "ruler" of such darkness as terrorist Saul, a Pharisee who zealously kept the law and persecuted the church, thinking he did God service [^3]. The law is the strength of sin (1Cor 15:56), and where law rules, darkness reigns because sin is imputed and death follows [^2].

The "darkness" Paul speaks of is the same "gross darkness" that shall cover the people in Isaiah 60:2, which is the result of mixing grace with law—a confusion that produces a "child of hell" (Mt 23:15) [^1]. These rulers are the spiritual wickedness in high places that promote the lie that law is necessary for righteousness, when in fact Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth (Rom 10:4) [^2]. The rulers of darkness are those who "hold the truth in unrighteousness" (Rom 1:18), suppressing the knowledge of God's grace by adding law to it, thereby making the grace of God of none effect [^1].

But Paul's purpose in revealing these rulers is not to frighten believers, but to equip them with the armor of light—the whole armor of God—which is grace [^1]. For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against these principalities and powers of law-based thinking. The victory is already won: Christ abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments (Eph 2:15), making peace [^2]. Therefore, we are to stand fast in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage (Gal 5:1) [^2]. The darkness has no power over those who are children of light, walking in the grace that reigns through righteousness unto eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord [^2].

Learn More:
- [God of all grace is not law, nor both law & grace, but all grace](/pages/god_of_all_grace_is_not_law_nor_both_law_&_grace_but_all_grace)
- [Some Apostle Paul mentions about the law](/pages/some_apostle_paul_mentions_about_the_law)
- [Conversion of Paul The Apostle Paul](/pages/paultheapostle)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
